framework: Schelling focal point detection + physical observables
design: 两层观测: 物理层(状态)+ 焦点层(转折). History in geopulse.db

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), IRGC claims 2nd F-35 shot down Apr 3, 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained, CENTCOM Wk5 12,300+ sites
history.note: Day 45. ★★★★★ IRGC F-35 CLAIM + IRAN-OMAN PERMANENT PROTOCOL: (1) IRGC claims 2nd F-35 shot down over central Tehran Apr 3 2:50AM — Raad AD system or adapted air-to-air missile (SCMP Chinese analysts); Pentagon NOT confirmed; (2) CENTCOM Wk5: 12,300+ sites struck, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' (VoiceOfEmirates Apr 2); (3) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day — Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo w/ Houthis+Hezbollah CONFIRMED one-off; (4) US intel: Iran STILL has 'significant missile-launching capability', can 'wreak absolute havoc' (Moneycontrol Apr 3); (5) Houthis SUSTAINED — BM launches at Israel, threatening Bab el-Mandeb closure if escalation continues (TWZ/Wikipedia Apr 2-3); (6) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT front; (7) US struck Karaj bridge Apr 2 (8 killed, 95 wounded) + medical center + steel plants = CIVILIAN infrastructure targeting (Guardian/BBC/Moneycontrol); (8) Iran+allies TRADED FIRE with Israel/US Apr 3 — energy+civilian infrastructure targeted (TheHindu/Outlook); (9) US KIA: 15 confirmed + 520+ wounded (Intercept: Pentagon COVERING UP); (10) Iran: HRANA 7,007+ deaths documented. ★★★ CRITICAL: 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual + Houthis sustained + Hezbollah 5K+). Air campaign Wk5 FAILING to eliminate Iran strike capability (Hegseth concession). IRGC F-35 claim = Iran AD NOT destroyed. Source: 24NewsHD/News18/SCMP/VoiceOfEmirates/Moneycontrol/TWZ/Wikipedia/Guardian/BBC/Intercept/TheHindu/Outlook

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), ★★★ US F-15E CONFIRMED shot down over Iran Apr 3 — first US aircraft loss, S&R underway, pilot fate unknown; IRGC claimed F-35 but wreckage shows F-15E tail sections (TWZ); multi-front fire exchange SUSTAINED
history.note: Day 36/Day 46. ★★★★★ US F-15E CONFIRMED SHOT DOWN + MULTI-FRONT SUSTAINED: (1) ★★★ BREAKING: US F-15E fighter jet CONFIRMED shot down over Iran Apr 3 — NYT/Reuters/CBS/Guardian all confirm; US S&R mission underway, pilot fate unknown; IRGC claimed F-35 but analysts ID wreckage as F-15E tail sections (TWZ/TurkiyeToday); (2) AP: Iran state TV affiliate says pilot ejected over southwestern Iran, locals converging on crash site, IRGC calls on public not to mistreat pilot; (3) Tasnim (IRGC-linked): US rescue attempts have FAILED; (4) CBS: low-altitude US aircraft seen over hostile territory = rescue ops; (5) NYT: US death toll 13 combat + 2 non-combat = 15 KIA + hundreds wounded; (6) CENTCOM Wk5: 12,300+ sites struck, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' (VoiceOfEmirates); (7) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day; Apr 1 joint 100+ salvo w/ Houthis+Hezbollah = one-off; (8) Houthis SUSTAINED — independent BM launches at Israel; (9) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (10) Iran+allies TRADED FIRE across Mideast Apr 3, targets incl energy+civilian infrastructure (AP/TheMessenger). ★★★ CRITICAL: F-15E shootdown = Iran AD NOT eliminated after 5+ weeks bombing. 3 INDEPENDENT fronts confirmed active. First confirmed US aircraft loss = massive political shock. Source: NYT/Reuters/CBS/Guardian/TWZ/AP/TurkiyeToday/VoiceOfEmirates/Tasnim

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), ★★★ F-15E CONFIRMED shot down Apr 3 = first US aircraft loss; one pilot rescued, one missing; CENTCOM Wk5 12,300+ sites; Apr 2 IRGC intercepted 26 drones+9 BM (UAE) + 13 drones+2 CM (Kuwait) + more (Bahrain); 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained
history.note: Day 46. ★★★★★ F-15E LOSS + MULTI-FRONT SUSTAINED: (1) ★★★ BREAKING: US F-15E shot down over Iran Apr 3 — NYT confirms one pilot rescued, other missing; IRGC claimed F-35 but analysts ID F-15E (TWZ); (2) ISW Apr 2: Emirati MoD intercepted 26 drones+9 BM; Kuwait 13 drones+2 CM; Bahrain intercepted drones+BM — coordinated IRGC multi-front salvo vs Gulf bases Apr 2; (3) CENTCOM Wk5: 12,300+ sites struck, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' BUT F-15E loss contradicts; (4) IRGC baseline 1-2/day RESIDUAL but coordinated salvos PERSIST; (5) Houthis SUSTAINED — independent BM launches at Israel; (6) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (7) US KIA: 13-15 confirmed + hundreds wounded (NYT/Wikipedia/Intercept); (8) Iran: HRANA 7,007+ deaths documented; (9) Trump: '2-3 more extremely hard weeks'; (10) Iran AD NOT eliminated — F-15E shootdown proves capability retained. PATTERN: 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual+coordinated salvos, Houthis sustained, Hezbollah 5K+). F-15E loss = biggest US military shock since war start. Source: NYT/Reuters/CBS/Guardian/ISW/TWZ/Wikipedia/Intercept

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), ★★★ TWO US AIRCRAFT LOST Apr 3: F-15E shot down over western Iran (494th FS) + A-10 Warthog shot down near Hormuz (Persian Gulf); 2 CSAR helicopters also hit; F-15E pilot rescued, WSO MISSING — IRGC/Army AD claims; Israel SUSPENDED airstrikes for rescue ops; Iran REJECTED US 48-hour ceasefire proposal via third country; CENTCOM Wk5 12,300+ sites struck but Iran AD proven NOT eliminated
history.note: Day 47. ★★★★★ DOUBLE AIRCRAFT LOSS = PARADIGM SHIFT: (1) F-15E Strike Eagle (494th FS) shot down over western Iran — pilot rescued, WSO missing, IRGC/Army AD claims, ejection seat found near crash (Wikipedia/TWZ/WaPo/NYT/CBS/NBC); (2) A-10 Warthog ALSO shot down near Hormuz/Persian Gulf same timeframe — pilot rescued (NYT/WaPo/Forbes/Guardian); (3) TWO CSAR helicopters HIT during rescue ops, crews injured but returned to base (WaPo); (4) Israel SUSPENDED airstrikes in areas 'relevant' to rescue (CBS/CNBCTV18); (5) Iran REJECTED US 48-hour ceasefire proposal via third country (Reuters/Fars/France24); (6) CENTCOM Wk5: 12,300+ sites struck, Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' BUT double shootdown CONTRADICTS; (7) UNSC Hormuz vote FURTHER DELAYED to next week (Reuters); China opposes force authorization; (8) ★★★ FIRST WESTERN HORMUZ TRANSITS: French CMA CGM Kribi + Japanese tanker + Omani vessels crossed Hormuz Apr 3 — first Western European transit since war (Bloomberg/Reuters/AJE/Guardian/Euronews); (9) 3 Omani tankers used NEW Oman coast route BYPASSING Iranian corridor (Bloomberg); (10) Good Friday — US stock markets CLOSED. Source: NYT/WaPo/Reuters/CBS/NBC/Forbes/TWZ/Bloomberg/AJE/Guardian/Euronews/France24

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), ★★★ TWO US aircraft (F-15E+A-10) shot down Apr 3 = first US aircraft losses; Iran REJECTED US 48-hr ceasefire; CENTCOM Wk5 12,300+ sites; Apr 2 IRGC salvos vs UAE(26drones+9BM)/Kuwait(13+2CM)/Bahrain/Qatar; 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained
history.note: Day 37. ★★★★★ POST-SHOOTDOWN + CEASEFIRE REJECTED: (1) CENTCOM Apr 2 video: strikes on drones/tanks/launchers, ~6,000 targets in Op Epic Fury (AJE/CENTCOM); (2) ISW Apr 2: Iran launched coordinated multi-front salvo vs Gulf states — UAE intercepted 26 drones+9 BM, Kuwait 13 drones+2 CM, Bahrain intercepted drones+missiles, Qatar 3 CM (1 struck QatarEnergy tanker); (3) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day; (4) TWO US aircraft lost Apr 3: F-15E Strike Eagle (494th FS) over western Iran + A-10 Warthog near Hormuz — first confirmed US aircraft losses; pilot rescued, WSO missing; (5) Iran REJECTED US 48-hour ceasefire proposal via third country (Reuters/Fars Apr 3); (6) Houthis SUSTAINED — entered war Mar 28, BM+drones at Israel, warned of Bab el-Mandeb closure (Bloomberg/Wikipedia); (7) Hezbollah INDEPENDENT — 71 attacks targeting Israel Mar 31-Apr 1, shot down IAF drone (ISW Apr 1); 5,000+ since Mar 2; (8) US KIA: 15 confirmed + hundreds wounded (NYT/Intercept — Pentagon accused of coverup); (9) Iran: HRANA 7,007+ deaths; (10) Trump Apr 1: demands Iran negotiate or face escalation, claims Iran asked for ceasefire, US precondition = Hormuz open. ★★★ CRITICAL: 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual+coordinated salvos, Houthis sustained, Hezbollah 5K+). F-15E+A-10 shootdowns = Iran AD NOT eliminated after 5 weeks. Source: AJE/CENTCOM/ISW/NYT/Reuters/Fars/Bloomberg/Wikipedia/Intercept/Guardian

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), ★★★ TWO US aircraft (F-15E+A-10) shot down Apr 3 CONFIRMED = first US aircraft losses; WSO STILL MISSING; Trump threatens bridges+power plants Apr 3 (Reuters); Apr 6 energy deadline T-2 DAYS; CENTCOM Wk5+ 12,300+ sites; 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained (IRGC residual+Houthis+Hezbollah 5K+)
history.note: Day 38/Day 48. ★★★★★ POST-DOUBLE SHOOTDOWN + APR 6 DEADLINE T-2: (1) F-15E Strike Eagle (494th FS) shot down over western Iran Apr 3 — pilot rescued, WSO STILL MISSING; A-10 Warthog also shot down near Hormuz — pilot rescued (NYT/WaPo/CBS/NBC/Military.com); (2) TWO CSAR helicopters HIT during rescue, crews wounded but returned to base (WaPo/CBS); (3) Israel SUSPENDED airstrikes in areas 'relevant' to rescue (CBS); (4) Trump Apr 3: threatens to destroy bridges+power plants (Reuters); (5) Apr 6 8PM ET energy deadline = T-2 DAYS — if Hormuz not reopened, US strikes energy infrastructure (JagoNews/EnergyConnects/PrismNews); (6) CENTCOM Wk5+: 12,300+ sites struck but Iran AD PROVEN NOT ELIMINATED (F-15E+A-10 losses); (7) IRGC struck Emirates Global Aluminium Abu Dhabi — significant damage, forced shutdown (Haaretz Apr 3); (8) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day; (9) Houthis SUSTAINED — BM at Israel, independent front; (10) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT. ★★★ CRITICAL: Apr 6 deadline = 2 days. Double aircraft loss = Iran AD NOT destroyed after 5+ weeks. 3 independent fronts active. WSO missing = political pressure multiplier. Source: NYT/WaPo/CBS/NBC/Military.com/Reuters/Haaretz/JagoNews/EnergyConnects

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), Apr 2 joint Iran/Houthi/Hezbollah salvo at Israel (5 wounded); CENTCOM Wk5+ 12,300+ sites; F-15E+A-10 shot down Apr 3 = first US aircraft losses; WSO STILL MISSING; IRGC commander killed Khorramabad (Fars); 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained
history.note: Day 48. ★★★★★ MULTI-FRONT SUSTAINED + CHINA 5-POINT PLAN: (1) CENTCOM Wk5+: 12,300+ sites struck; Cooper: 'substantial portion neutralized' BUT F-15E+A-10 losses CONTRADICT; (2) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day declining; Apr 2 joint Iran+Houthi+Hezbollah salvo at Israel — 5 wounded (Haaretz Apr 2); (3) F-15E Strike Eagle (494th FS) + A-10 Warthog BOTH shot down Apr 3 = FIRST US aircraft losses; one pilot rescued, WSO MISSING; 2 CSAR helos hit (NYT/WaPo/CBS); (4) IRGC commander killed in Khorramabad strike (Fars Apr 3); (5) Iran REJECTED US 48-hr ceasefire via third country (Reuters/Fars Apr 3); (6) Trump Apr 1: claims Iran asked for ceasefire, precondition = Hormuz open (Reuters); Iran FM: 'no negotiation' (CNBC); (7) Houthis SUSTAINED — joint BM+drones at Israel since Mar 28, warned of Bab el-Mandeb closure (Wikipedia); (8) Hezbollah 5,000+ since Mar 2 — INDEPENDENT; (9) ★★★ China 5-point proposal with Pakistan ACTIVE — envoy Zhai Jun 20+ calls with regional FMs, special envoy visiting region; US 'appears uninterested' (AP/CNN Apr 3-4); (10) War Powers resolution gaining House support — Dems say enough votes to pass (TheHill Apr 2). Source: Reuters/Haaretz/NYT/WaPo/CBS/Fars/CNBC/AP/CNN/TheHill/Wikipedia

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), ★★★ BUSHEHR NUCLEAR PERIMETER HIT Apr 4 (1 killed, IAEA confirmed no radiation rise); Mahshahr Petrochemical Zone STRUCK Apr 4 = energy infrastructure targeting NOW ACTIVE; F-15E+A-10 shot down Apr 3 CONFIRMED; WSO STILL MISSING; Trump '48 hours before all hell'; 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained
history.note: Day 49. ★★★★★ BUSHEHR NUCLEAR THRESHOLD + PETROCHEMICAL STRIKES: (1) ★★★ BREAKING: Projectile struck PERIMETER of Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant Apr 4 — 1 worker killed (Tasnim/IAEA/NYT/AJE); IAEA confirmed no radiation increase; Iran informed IAEA; (2) ★★★ Mahshahr Petrochemical Special Economic Zone STRUCK Apr 4 — multiple explosions, smoke rising across zone (AJE/NYT/Fars); (3) US/Israel now targeting petrochemical+nuclear-adjacent infrastructure BEFORE Apr 6 power plant deadline; (4) F-15E Strike Eagle (494th FS) + A-10 Warthog BOTH shot down Apr 3 = FIRST US aircraft losses; WSO STILL MISSING (NYT/WaPo/CBS); (5) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day declining; (6) Iran fired missiles at Israel Apr 3 (HT); (7) Trump: '48 hours before all hell' (Sky News) WHILE postponing power plant strikes 5 more days; (8) CENTCOM Wk5+: 12,300+ sites struck; (9) US KIA: 13-15 confirmed + hundreds wounded (NYT); (10) Iran: 2,076+ killed, 26,500 wounded (AJE Apr 3). ★★★ CRITICAL: Petrochemical strikes + Bushehr perimeter = energy targeting HAS BEGUN before official Apr 6 deadline. 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual, Houthis sustained, Hezbollah 5K+). Source: NYT/AJE/IAEA/Tasnim/Fars/Sky/WaPo/CBS/HT

history.est: 1-2/day declining (IRGC baseline), ★★★ Bushehr nuclear perimeter+Mahshahr petrochemical STRUCK Apr 4; explosions reported Apr 4 at auxiliary Bushehr building+Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Zone; CENTCOM Wk5+ ~6,000 targets in Op Epic Fury; F-15E+A-10 shot down Apr 3 CONFIRMED; WSO STILL MISSING; 3 INDEPENDENT fronts sustained (IRGC residual+Houthis+Hezbollah)
history.note: Day 50. ★★★★★ NUCLEAR PERIMETER + PETROCHEMICAL INFRASTRUCTURE TARGETED: (1) Apr 4: Explosions at Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant auxiliary building + Mahshahr Special Petrochemical Zone (Wikipedia/AJE/NYT); 1 worker killed at Bushehr perimeter; IAEA confirmed no radiation increase; (2) CENTCOM Wk5+: ~6,000 targets struck in Op Epic Fury (AJE/CENTCOM); (3) IRGC baseline STILL 1-2/day declining; Apr 2 joint Iran+Houthi+Hezbollah salvo at Israel = coordinated show-of-force; (4) F-15E Strike Eagle (494th FS) + A-10 Warthog BOTH shot down Apr 3 = FIRST US aircraft losses; pilot rescued, WSO MISSING; Iran offered $60K reward for WSO; (5) Houthis SUSTAINED — 4+ attacks on Israel since entering war Mar 28, joint BM+drones; warned of Bab el-Mandeb closure; (6) Hezbollah 5,000+ attacks since Mar 2 — 71 attacks targeting Israel Mar 31-Apr 1 alone; shot down IAF drone; INDEPENDENT front; (7) US KIA: 15 confirmed + hundreds wounded; Pentagon accused of coverup (Intercept); (8) Iran: 7,007+ deaths (HRANA); (9) Trump '48 hours before all hell' WHILE postponing power plant strikes 5 days; (10) ★★★ CRITICAL: Energy/nuclear infrastructure targeting NOW ACTIVE before official Apr 6 deadline. 3 INDEPENDENT fronts (IRGC residual, Houthis sustained, Hezbollah 5K+). Iran AD PROVEN NOT eliminated (F-15E+A-10 losses). Source: Wikipedia/AJE/CENTCOM/NYT/Intercept/IAEA/Bloomberg

history.note: Action-only: CENTCOM and major-wire war updates still indicate recurring strike activity with no verified zero-attack window; maintain low-but-persistent baseline.

history.est: 1-2/day (low persistent)
history.note: Action-only: CENTCOM and major outlets still report recurring strike activity (e.g., week-5 strike footage, continued exchanges); no verified zero-attack window.

history.est: 1-2 incidents/day
history.note: Action-filtered: CENTCOM continues daily strike releases (latest ~14h), Reuters reports near-daily escort requests amid recurring ship attacks/harassment; tempo remains sustained rather than spiking.

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-5 ships/day), Iran-Oman drafting PERMANENT Hormuz 'monitoring' protocol, Windward Apr 1: ~11 ships (1T+4C in / 1T+5C out), NO US escort Wk5+
history.note: Day 45. ★★★★★ IRAN PERMANENT SOVEREIGNTY ESCALATION: (1) ★★★ CNBC/IRNA Apr 3: Iran+Oman drafting protocol to 'monitor transit' through Hormuz — Deputy FM Gharibabadi: tanker traffic 'should be supervised and coordinated' with Iran+Oman = PERMANENT peacetime control bid; (2) MS.NOW: Iran moves to take 'permanent control' of Hormuz; (3) Windward Apr 1 daily: 1 tanker + 4 cargo inbound, 1 tanker + 5 cargo outbound = ~11 ships/day through Hormuz (vs 150/day pre-crisis); (4) UANI Apr 1: OFAC-sanctioned tankers transiting with AIS off; (5) Friendly-nation list EXPANDING: PH/MY/TH added (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK); (6) Iran Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED — IRGC vets+escorts each ship, USD2M toll, yuan+crypto; (7) Iran parliament CODIFIED tolls legislation; (8) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts' — 5+ weeks; (9) FPRI Apr 2: Hormuz campaign requires 1-2 warships per escort + mine countermeasures; (10) 31st MEU deployed but NO escorts materialized. ★★★ TWO-TIER HARDENING INTO PERMANENT REGIME: Iran-Oman protocol = attempt to codify wartime control into peacetime international arrangement. Still 95%+ below pre-crisis. Source: CNBC/IRNA/MS.NOW/Windward/UANI/USNI/FPRI/Wikipedia

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-5 ships/day), UNSC Hormuz vote DELAYED to Saturday, Bahrain waters down to 'defensive only', Russia/China/France opposed force, Iran-Oman permanent protocol drafting
history.note: Day 46. ★★★★★ UNSC HORMUZ VOTE DELAYED + DEFENSIVE ONLY: (1) AP: Bahrain WATERS DOWN UNSC Hormuz resolution — from 'all necessary means' to 'all DEFENSIVE means necessary and commensurate' = NO offensive military action authorized (AP Apr 3); (2) DW: UNSC vote DELAYED from Friday to Saturday (originally Apr 4 → Apr 5); (3) Russia/China/France all opposed original force authorization — China: 'unlawful and indiscriminate'; Russia: 'does not solve the puzzle'; France: indicated watered-down version 'might be acceptable'; (4) Final draft: 6-month defensive mandate, countries must notify UNSC in advance; (5) DW: ship transit 'slightly up but still far below pre-war levels'; (6) Polymarket: 98.3% for 0-10 ships Apr 3; (7) Iran-Oman PERMANENT monitoring protocol still in drafting (CNBC/IRNA); (8) Philippines added to friendly-nation list Apr 2 (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK/MY/TH); (9) Iran Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED — IRGC vets+escorts, USD2M toll; (10) USNI: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts' — 5+ weeks. ★★★ UNSC vote = first multilateral action attempt but WATERED DOWN. Still 95%+ below pre-crisis. Source: AP/DW/CNBC/IRNA/Polymarket/USNI

history.est: ~3-4 mbd (4-5 ships/day), UNSC Hormuz vote DELAYED to Sat, Bahrain resolution watered down to 'defensive only', Iran-Oman permanent Hormuz protocol drafting, NO US escort Wk5+
history.note: Day 46. ★★★★★ HORMUZ VOTE DELAYED + IRAN-OMAN PROTOCOL: (1) DW: UNSC vote DELAYED to Saturday — Bahrain resolution WATERED DOWN from 'all necessary means' to 'all defensive means' (AP Apr 3); Russia/China/France opposed force; France may accept defensive version; (2) Iran-Oman drafting PERMANENT 'monitoring' protocol for Hormuz transit = peacetime sovereignty bid (CNBC/IRNA); (3) Gulf Business/Lloyd's: 181 vessels total Mar 1-30 vs ~4,500 same period 2025 = 96% collapse; (4) USNI: 142 transits Mar 1-25 vs 2,652 same period 2025; (5) Friendly-nation list expanding: PH/MY/TH added (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK); (6) Iran Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED — IRGC vets+escorts, USD2M toll; (7) Oman coast bypass route emerged (Bloomberg); (8) Iran-Oman protocol + UNSC vote Sat = competing frameworks; (9) USNI: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts' — 5+ weeks; (10) S&P VOO closed +1.5% on Iran-Oman Hormuz shipping protocol news. Still 95%+ below pre-crisis. Source: AP/DW/GulfBusiness/Lloyd's/USNI/CNBC/IRNA/Bloomberg/TipRanks

history.est: ~3-5 mbd (5-8 ships/day INCREASING), ★★★ FIRST WESTERN TRANSITS: French CMA CGM Kribi + Japanese-owned tanker crossed Hormuz Apr 3 = first Western European transit since war; 3 Omani tankers via NEW coast bypass route; friendly-nation list expanding (PH/MY/TH); Iran Larak checkpoint + USD2M toll STILL enforced; UNSC vote delayed to next week
history.note: Day 47. ★★★★★ WESTERN TRANSITS = PARTIAL CRACK IN BLOCKADE: (1) ★★★ Bloomberg/Reuters/AJE/Guardian Apr 3: French CMA CGM Kribi (Maltese-flagged) transited Hormuz EASTBOUND from Dubai — FIRST Western European vessel since war began; (2) Japanese-owned tanker also crossed (Bloomberg); (3) 3 Omani-owned tankers (Dhalkut, Habrut + Sohar LNG) used NEW route hugging Oman coastline BYPASSING Iranian corridor (Bloomberg Apr 2); (4) Macron: only diplomacy not military can open Hormuz — CMA CGM transit = French diplomatic signal; (5) Friendly-nation list EXPANDED: Philippines Apr 2 (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK/MY/TH); (6) Iran Larak checkpoint STILL operating — IRGC vets+escorts, USD2M toll, yuan/crypto; (7) UNSC Hormuz vote DELAYED AGAIN to next week (Reuters Apr 3) — China opposes force; Bahrain resolution watered down to 'defensive only'; (8) Iran-Oman permanent monitoring protocol STILL in drafting (CNBC/IRNA); (9) Transit still 95%+ below pre-crisis 150/day BUT trend = incrementally EXPANDING; (10) Ships ~2,190+ waiting west of Hormuz (Bloomberg). Source: Bloomberg/Reuters/AJE/Guardian/Euronews/CNBC/IRNA

history.est: ~3-5 mbd (5-8 ships/day INCREASING), ★★★ FIRST WESTERN TRANSITS: French CMA CGM + Japanese tanker crossed Hormuz Apr 3; 3 Omani tankers via NEW coast bypass; Iran Larak checkpoint+USD2M toll enforced; UNSC vote delayed to next week; ~100 vessels late March (BBC ~5-6/day); Iran REJECTED 48-hr ceasefire
history.note: Day 37. ★★★★★ WESTERN CRACKS IN BLOCKADE + CEASEFIRE REJECTED: (1) BBC Apr 4: ~100 vessels passed Hormuz late March = ~5-6/day, roughly 1/3 Iran-linked; (2) French CMA CGM Kribi (Maltese-flagged) transited Apr 3 = FIRST Western European vessel since war (Bloomberg/Reuters/Guardian); (3) Japanese-owned tanker also crossed (Bloomberg); (4) 3 Omani tankers used NEW Oman coast route BYPASSING Iranian corridor (Bloomberg); (5) Guardian Mar 26: many willing vessels using alternative route through Iranian waters; (6) Iran Larak checkpoint FORMALIZED — IRGC vets each ship, USD2M toll, yuan+crypto; (7) Friendly-nation list EXPANDED: PH/MY/TH added (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK); (8) Iran-Oman drafting PERMANENT Hormuz monitoring protocol = peacetime sovereignty bid (NYT/CNBC); (9) UNSC vote DELAYED to next week — Bahrain resolution watered down to 'defensive only', China/Russia opposed force (DW/AP); (10) USNI Proceedings Apr: 'US has NOT undertaken naval escorts' — 5+ weeks unfulfilled; (11) ~2,190+ vessels waiting west of Hormuz; (12) 181 vessels Mar 1-30 vs ~4,500 same period 2025 = 96% collapse (Lloyd's). Still 95%+ below pre-crisis but trend INCREMENTALLY expanding. Source: BBC/Bloomberg/Reuters/Guardian/DW/AP/USNI/CNBC/Lloyd's

history.est: ~3-5 mbd (5-8 ships/day INCREMENTALLY INCREASING), ★★★ FIRST WESTERN TRANSITS: French CMA CGM Kribi + Japanese tanker crossed Apr 3; 3 Omani tankers via NEW Oman coast bypass; UNSC vote delayed to next week; Iran Larak checkpoint+USD2M toll enforced; ~100 vessels late March (BBC); still 95%+ below pre-crisis
history.note: Day 38. ★★★★★ WESTERN CRACKS + BYPASS ROUTE + UNSC DELAYED: (1) Bloomberg/Reuters/Guardian/SCMP: French CMA CGM Kribi (Maltese-flagged) transited Hormuz EASTBOUND Apr 3 = FIRST Western European vessel since war; (2) Japanese-owned tanker also crossed (Bloomberg); (3) 3 Omani tankers (Dhalkut/Habrut/Sohar LNG) used NEW Oman coast route BYPASSING Iranian corridor (Bloomberg/ProtoThema); (4) BBC: ~100 vessels passed Hormuz late March = ~5-6/day, ~1/3 Iran-linked; (5) UNSC Hormuz vote DELAYED to next week (Reuters Apr 3) — Bahrain resolution watered down to 'defensive only' (AP/WaPo/DW); China/Russia oppose force; France may accept defensive version; (6) Wikipedia: Iran friendly-nation list EXPANDED: PH Apr 2 (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK/MY/TH); (7) Iran-Oman PERMANENT monitoring protocol still drafting (NYT/CNBC); (8) Iran Larak checkpoint operating — IRGC vets+escorts, USD2M toll; (9) USNI Proceedings: US has NOT undertaken naval escorts — 5+ weeks unfulfilled; (10) ~2,190+ vessels waiting west of Hormuz. PATTERN: Incremental EXPANSION (Western+Oman bypass) but still 95%+ below pre-crisis 150/day. Source: Bloomberg/Reuters/Guardian/SCMP/BBC/AP/WaPo/DW/Euronews/USNI

history.est: ~3-5 mbd (5-8 ships/day INCREMENTALLY INCREASING), FIRST WESTERN TRANSITS Apr 3: French CMA CGM Kribi + Japanese tanker; 3 Omani tankers via NEW coast bypass; Hegseth downplays risk; Lloyd's: <10% of average; UNSC Hormuz vote delayed to next week; Iran Larak checkpoint+USD2M toll enforced
history.note: Day 48. ★★★★★ WESTERN CRACKS + HEGSETH DOWNPLAYS + UNSC DELAYED: (1) USNI Mar 31: Hegseth downplays risk of sailing through Hormuz as traffic 'slightly up'; Lloyd's: recent transits STILL <10% of average traffic; (2) French CMA CGM Kribi (Maltese-flagged) transited Hormuz EASTBOUND Apr 3 = FIRST Western European vessel since war (Bloomberg/Reuters/Guardian); (3) Japanese-owned tanker also crossed (Bloomberg); (4) 3 Omani tankers via NEW Oman coast bypass route (Bloomberg); (5) Guardian Mar 26: many vessels taking alternative route through Iranian waters — 'Tehran's tollbooth'; (6) UNSC vote DELAYED to next week — Bahrain resolution watered down to 'defensive only' (AP/DW Apr 3); China opposes force; (7) Iran Larak checkpoint operating — IRGC vets each ship, USD2M toll, yuan+crypto; (8) Friendly-nation list EXPANDED: PH/MY/TH (after CN/RU/IN/IQ/PK); (9) Iran-Oman permanent monitoring protocol drafting (NYT/CNBC); (10) ~2,190+ vessels waiting west of Hormuz; 181 total Mar 1-30 vs ~4,500 same period 2025 = 96% collapse (Lloyd's). Still 95%+ below pre-crisis but trend INCREMENTALLY expanding with Western cracks. Source: USNI/Bloomberg/Reuters/Guardian/AP/DW/NYT/CNBC/Lloyd's

history.est: ~1-1.5 mbd confirmed (DOE/Wright), D+30, deliveries ACTIVE since Apr 1, NEW 10M bbl Bryan Mound exchange RFP due Apr 6, SPR declining to ~243M bbl (40yr low)
history.note: Day 45 D+30. ★★★★ SPR ACTIVE BUT IRRELEVANT — EXCHANGE NOT SALE: (1) DOE Apr 1: NEW 10M bbl emergency EXCHANGE (not sale) from Bryan Mound site — companies repay 20% more barrels Nov 2026-Sep 2028 (Reuters/DOE/EnergiasMedia); (2) Rate confirmed 1-1.5 mbd (Wright/S&P Global); (3) SPR deliveries UNDERWAY since Apr 1 — Shell/Marathon/BP/Gunvor receiving; (4) SPR projected to decline from ~415M to ~243M bbl = lowest since early 1980s, ~34% of 714M capacity (Grokipedia); (5) WebProNews: DOE FIRMLY DENIED plans for further releases; (6) G7 coordinated 412M bbl (US 172M = bulk); (7) IEA Birol warns supply crunch WORSENING April (CNBC); (8) Gas USD4.06/gal national average Apr 1 = highest since 2022, +36% since war start, CA well over USD5 (SmartAsset/CNBC/NYT); (9) Brent futures USD108.95, spot cargo hit USD141.36 = 2008 high (Investing.com/CNBC); (10) WTI USD111.29 (OilPrice Apr 3). SPR trickle IRRELEVANT — 1.5 mbd vs 15-17 mbd Hormuz disruption = 10% replacement. Physical market pricing EXTREME scarcity despite SPR deliveries. Source: Reuters/DOE/EnergiasMedia/Grokipedia/WebProNews/CNBC/SmartAsset/NYT/Investing.com/OilPrice

fp1_china_action.label: 中国实质行动(唯一可行聚点注入)
fp1_china_action.findings: China shows diplomatic action (envoy shuttle + five-point consultations) but no high-confidence, independently confirmed PLA Navy operational move in Hormuz; substantive hard-power shift not yet confirmed.

fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.label: 美方信号包内部矛盾(承诺解锁前导)
fp2_us_signal_inconsistency.findings: Contradiction persists in action set: US explores/announces escort concepts while Navy reporting escort limits and warfighting risk; parallel White House negotiation signaling continues.

fp3_face_saving_framework.label: 面子工程原材料出现
fp3_face_saving_framework.findings: Framework signals exist (UNSC Resolution 2817, competing ceasefire proposals) but no accepted, operational deconfliction architecture yet.

fp4_cost_threshold.label: 成本阈值的物理逼近
fp4_cost_threshold.findings: Cost pressure rising (AAA gasoline ~4.10/gal, reported US casualties in low-teens, active War Powers filings) but no decisive congressional constraint or strategic rollback action yet.

noise_filter.rule: 按行为类型分类,不按人物分类
noise: 巴盖伊等发言人声明(言辞)
媒体'消息人士'报道(未验证)
signal: Trump签署行政令/军事命令(行动)
美军实际调兵/部署变化(行动)
IRGC实际攻击模式变化(行动)
中国实际经济/军事动作(行动)
noise_filter.key_distinction: 同一个人的言辞=噪声,行动=信号。Trump说'just for fun'=噪声。Trump命令打Kharg=信号。

pipeline_daily_checklist: 1. 物理层: 更新Q1(袭击频率) + Q2(通行量) + Q3(SPR释放)
2. 焦点层: 扫描fp1(中国动作) + fp2(美方矛盾) + fp3(退出框架) + fp4(成本阈值)
3. 噪声过滤: 新闻分类为action vs rhetoric
4. 结构性验证: 跑structural_verify检查盲区+停滞+链断裂
5. DAG更新: 只有物理层变化或焦点层信号触发才更新概率
